nodes.label: Jaime Maussan
nodes.summary: Mexican UFO journalist; central figure promoting the Peru tridactyl mummies and presenting them before the Mexican Congress.

sources.quote: Central figure promoting Peru tridactyl mummies

nodes.label: Leandro (gravedigger)
nodes.summary: Peruvian gravedigger who per local account stumbled upon a cave with ~200 tridactyl bodies near the Nazca region.

sources.quote: Peruvian gravedigger who discovered the mummy cave

nodes.label: Michael Mazzola
nodes.summary: American filmmaker producing 'Catastrophic Disclosure' documentary on the Peruvian tridactyl mummies.

sources.quote: Filmmaker of 'Catastrophic Disclosure' investigating the mummies

nodes.label: Ammar Kandil
nodes.summary: Co-founder of Yes Theory; accompanied Michels on the Peru mummies investigation trip.

nodes.label: John McDowell
nodes.summary: Forensic expert; won Association forensics award for analyzing the Peru tridactyl specimens.

sources.quote: Forensic expert corroborating the mummy analysis

nodes.label: Jim Caruso
nodes.summary: Chief medical examiner for Denver; Navy officer for decades; performed over 300 autopsies; reviewed the Peru mummy materials.

nodes.summary: President of the Peruvian association equivalent to the American Academy of Forensic Sciences; examined the tridactyl specimens.

nodes.label: Catastrophic Disclosure

sources.timestamp_end: Mazzola's documentary on the Peru mummies
Southern Peruvian region where mummies were found

nodes.label: Tridactyl Mummy
nodes.summary: Class of mummified humanoid bodies found near Nazca, Peru with three fingers and three toes; two physical types (M-type hominids and smaller reptilians). Radiocarbon dates overlap Nazca period. Status contested.

sources.timestamp_end: Three-fingered/three-toed humanoid bodies preserved in diatomaceous earth

nodes.label: Per Leandro's account and subsequent investigation, a cave near Nazca Peru contained ~200 tridactyl
nodes.summary: Per Leandro's account and subsequent investigation, a cave near Nazca Peru contained ~200 tridactyl bodies preserved in diatomaceous earth.

sources.quote: Big clumps began to emerge — heads, limbs, and appendages, in many cases covered in diatomaceous earth. Pile of 200 bodies and parts.

type_specific.statement_text: Per Leandro's account and subsequent investigation, a cave near Nazca Peru contained ~200 tridactyl bodies preserved in diatomaceous earth.

nodes.label: Two distinct tridactyl mummy types have been identified: M-types (larger hominid morphology, 25-30 s
nodes.summary: Two distinct tridactyl mummy types have been identified: M-types (larger hominid morphology, 25-30 specimens) and smaller reptilians (2-3 feet, Close-Encounters-like faces).

sources.quote: The M types or hominids. These look like — we have 25 to 30 of these. Reptilians. These are skinny, two to three feet. Their faces look like the aliens in Close Encounters.

type_specific.statement_text: Two distinct tridactyl mummy types have been identified: M-types (larger hominid morphology, 25-30 specimens) and smaller reptilians (2-3 feet, Close-Encounters-like faces).

nodes.label: John McDowell won the Association for Forensics' Grand Wall Award for his analysis suggesting some s
nodes.summary: John McDowell won the Association for Forensics' Grand Wall Award for his analysis suggesting some specimens represent living organisms worthy of investigation.

sources.quote: John McDow association for a year and just won the forensics the Grand Wall Award. Data to show that some of these bodies to be perhaps living organisms.

type_specific.statement_text: John McDowell won the Association for Forensics' Grand Wall Award for his analysis suggesting some specimens represent living organisms worthy of investigation.

nodes.label: Denver chief medical examiner Jim Caruso (Navy veteran, 300+ autopsies) has publicly defended the re
nodes.summary: Denver chief medical examiner Jim Caruso (Navy veteran, 300+ autopsies) has publicly defended the reality of the specimens as biological rather than fabricated.

sources.quote: Jim Caruso, the chief medical examiner for Denver, officer in the Navy for decades, Caruso has personally performed over 300 autopsies.

type_specific.statement_text: Denver chief medical examiner Jim Caruso (Navy veteran, 300+ autopsies) has publicly defended the reality of the specimens as biological rather than fabricated.

nodes.label: The Peruvian Ministry of Culture has, in multiple instances, allegedly acted to suppress the case an
nodes.summary: The Peruvian Ministry of Culture has, in multiple instances, allegedly acted to suppress the case and confiscate specimens at the Lima airport (2023).

sources.quote: Ministry of Culture in multiple instances — they don't want this case to see the light of day. These little trinkets, pieces of touristic were confiscated at the Lima airport in 2023.

type_specific.statement_text: The Peruvian Ministry of Culture has, in multiple instances, allegedly acted to suppress the case and confiscate specimens at the Lima airport (2023).

nodes.label: Some publicly-exhibited specimens (Reuters coverage via Flavio Estrada) were independently confirmed
nodes.summary: Some publicly-exhibited specimens (Reuters coverage via Flavio Estrada) were independently confirmed as dolls made from modern synthetic glue — Michels argues this is not a blanket debunk of the anatomically-consistent specimens.

sources.quote: Reuters: not extraterrestrials. They're dolls made from modern synthetic glue, said Flavio Estrada. But figurines and these real anatomically consistent — it's at that point that this story is fake.

type_specific.statement_text: Some publicly-exhibited specimens (Reuters coverage via Flavio Estrada) were independently confirmed as dolls made from modern synthetic glue — Michels argues this is not a blanket debunk of the anatomically-consistent specimens.

nodes.label: One of the tridactyl specimens contains a fetus inside, supporting (per researchers) that it was a b
nodes.summary: One of the tridactyl specimens contains a fetus inside, supporting (per researchers) that it was a biologically functioning female organism rather than an assemblage.

sources.quote: It has a fetus inside the — these features are also tractor.

type_specific.statement_text: One of the tridactyl specimens contains a fetus inside, supporting (per researchers) that it was a biologically functioning female organism rather than an assemblage.

nodes.label: Ancient Peruvian cranial-deformation rituals involved reshaping infant skulls to imitate elongated s
nodes.summary: Ancient Peruvian cranial-deformation rituals involved reshaping infant skulls to imitate elongated skulls attributed to NHI visitors, implying contact memory encoded in Nazca-era practice.

sources.quote: Cranial deformation, a deliberate reshaping of the skull often done to infants. Rituals conferred status. To be an attempt to imitate the gods.

type_specific.statement_text: Ancient Peruvian cranial-deformation rituals involved reshaping infant skulls to imitate elongated skulls attributed to NHI visitors, implying contact memory encoded in Nazca-era practice.

# Peru’s Three Fingered “Aliens” (WTF Are These?!) https://www.you
Episode: Peru’s Three Fingered “Aliens” (WTF Are These?!)
Per Leandro's account and subsequent investigation, a cave near Nazca Peru contained ~200 tridactyl bodies preserved in diatomaceous earth.
Two distinct tridactyl mummy types have been identified: M-types (larger hominid morphology, 25-30 specimens) and smaller reptilians (2-3 feet, Close-Encounters-like faces).
John McDowell won the Association for Forensics' Grand Wall Award for his analysis suggesting some specimens represent living organisms worthy of investigation.
Denver chief medical examiner Jim Caruso (Navy veteran, 300+ autopsies) has publicly defended the reality of the specimens as biological rather than fabricated.
The Peruvian Ministry of Culture has, in multiple instances, allegedly acted to suppress the case and confiscate specimens at the Lima airport (2023).

Some publicly-exhibited specimens (Reuters coverage via Flavio Estrada) were independently confirmed as dolls made from modern synthetic glue — Michels argues this is not a blanket debunk of the anato
One of the tridactyl specimens contains a fetus inside, supporting (per researchers) that it was a biologically functioning female organism rather than an assemblage.
Ancient Peruvian cranial-deformation rituals involved reshaping infant skulls to imitate elongated skulls attributed to NHI visitors, implying contact memory encoded in Nazca-era practice.
